title: Third-Party Access Governance Audit
summary: Assess whether vendor access is approved, time-bound, monitored, and removed promptly after business need ends.
objective: Assess whether third-party access to internal systems is approved, time-bound, monitored, and removed in accordance with vendor governance requirements.
scope: Timely removal of inactive vendor accounts

controls.name: Approved Vendor Access Requests
controls.description: Vendor accounts should be provisioned only after formal business approval.

controls.name: Time-Bound Third-Party Access
controls.description: Vendor access should expire automatically based on approved engagement duration.

controls.name: Monitoring of Vendor Privileged Activity
controls.description: Privileged third-party sessions should be logged and monitored.

controls.name: Removal of Inactive Vendor Accounts
controls.description: Inactive or ended vendor accounts should be removed promptly.

evidence.title: Vendor Access Request Form
evidence.content: Request form for Delta Support access includes technical details but the business owner approval signature is blank.

evidence.title: VPN Vendor Account Listing
evidence.content: VPN listing shows account delta_support_admin remains active 63 days after the contracted support window ended.

evidence.title: Privileged Session Log Export
evidence.content: Export for vendor sessions shows multiple administrator logins from the vendor account, but command logging is disabled for the maintenance bastion.

evidence.title: Contract End Notice
evidence.content: Procurement notice confirms the Delta Support engagement ended on January 31 and no extension was approved.

evidence.title: Security Review Memo
evidence.content: Security review memo requires all vendor admin access to be time-bound and monitored through the bastion recording feature.

evidence.title: Third-Party Access Standard
evidence.content: The standard requires business approval, defined expiry, monitored privileged access, and prompt deprovisioning of third-party accounts.

evidence.title: Active Vendor Roster
evidence.content: The current vendor roster lists active third parties and their contract end dates, including a routine review marker for upcoming renewals.

issues.title: Vendor Access Provisioned Without Business Approval
issues.description: A vendor access request was processed without documented business-owner approval.
issues.recommendation: Require completed business approval before provisioning third-party access.

issues.title: Vendor Access Not Time-Bound or Removed Promptly
issues.description: A vendor administrative account remained active well past the end of the approved engagement window.
issues.recommendation: Implement automatic expiry and periodic review for vendor accounts tied to contract end dates.

issues.title: Privileged Vendor Sessions Not Fully Monitored
issues.description: Vendor privileged activity occurred without complete command-level logging on the bastion host.
issues.recommendation: Enable full session recording and command logging for all vendor privileged access paths.

inbox.subject: Kickoff: Third-Party Access Governance
inbox.preview: Test whether vendor access really ends when the contract ends and whether high-risk sessions are watched closely enough.
inbox.body: The focus is third-party risk in practice: approval, expiry, monitoring, and deprovisioning. Pay special attention to whether technical controls actually enforce the policy promises.

inbox.subject: Vendor access records
inbox.preview: Sharing the Delta Support request, contract end note, and current access standard.
inbox.body: Delta Support previously handled urgent maintenance tasks, so some access may have remained available for convenience. Let me know what else you need.

interviewPrompts.question: Was Delta Support approved by the business owner before access was created?
interviewPrompts.answer: The technical request moved ahead first because the maintenance was urgent. We expected the owner to sign later, but that never happened.

interviewPrompts.question: Did the support contract get extended beyond January 31?
interviewPrompts.answer: No. Procurement closed the engagement at the end of January and no extension was approved.

interviewPrompts.question: Why is the vendor VPN account still active?
interviewPrompts.answer: We left it enabled in case maintenance work came back. There is no automatic expiry tied to the contract end date.

interviewPrompts.question: Are vendor admin sessions fully monitored through the bastion?
interviewPrompts.answer: They should be, but the command capture setting was disabled during troubleshooting and not restored afterwards.

closeout.managementMessage: Third-party access controls are defined, but enforcement over approvals, expiry, and monitoring is inconsistent. Vendor access needs stronger lifecycle control to reduce avoidable external access risk.
closeout.auditorReflection: Vendor access audits reward lifecycle thinking. The risk usually sits in the gap between contract dates, active accounts, and whether anyone is really watching privileged activity.
